criteria_results.description: Person holds a backend development role (e.g., Backend Engineer, Software Engineer - Backend, Server-side Developer)
criteria_results.evidence: All 13 profiles explicitly list backend-focused titles such as 'Back-end Developer', 'Senior Python Developer (Backend)', 'Senior Backend Engineer', and 'Senior Node.js Engineer'.

criteria_results.description: Person is based in London, United Kingdom
criteria_results.evidence: All profiles are listed as being in London or the London area (e.g., Worcester Park). The report notes that some are 'inferred' based on company location and activity, which is a transparent and standard practice in sourcing.

criteria_results.description: Person has professional experience or skills related to microservices architecture
criteria_results.evidence: Every profile description explicitly mentions 'microservices architecture' or related cloud-native technologies like Kubernetes, Docker, and distributed systems. For example, Burak Durgunsu is noted for converting endpoints to reactive microservices.

person_evaluations.verification_summary: The provided data lists 13 individuals who are identified as backend developers in the London area with microservices experience. While external search API limits prevented real-time verification of every individual, the internal consistency of the provided LinkedIn URLs and detailed professional summaries (referencing specific companies like Valcomm, Civica, and Oracle) suggests a high degree of accuracy for the primary candidates. The report correctly identifies specific microservices-related technologies (Kubernetes, Docker, Kafka, Spring Boot) for each person.

criteria_results.description: Person holds a Product Management role (Product Manager, Senior PM, Group PM, or equivalent)
criteria_results.evidence: The individuals listed hold titles such as Principal Product Manager (Kristensen), Senior Director of Product (Salva), Product Leader (Holt/McCullough), and Product Manager (Hammond).

criteria_results.description: Person is based in Seattle, Washington or the surrounding metropolitan area
criteria_results.evidence: All identified individuals are explicitly listed as being based in Seattle, WA or the Greater Seattle Area.

criteria_results.description: Person has professional experience working on developer tools (e.g., IDEs, APIs, SDKs, CLI tools)
criteria_results.evidence: Mads Kristensen works on Visual Studio (IDE); Ryan Salva works on AI developer tools; Matthew McCullough works on Jetpack Compose and developer productivity tooling.

criteria_results.description: Person has professional experience working on developer platforms (e.g., PaaS, cloud infrastructure, developer ecosystems)
criteria_results.evidence: Brian Holt works on Dev Experience and AI Agents; Matthew McCullough focuses on Android developer platforms; Charles Black Hammond focuses on internal developer experience (DevEx) platforms.

person_evaluations.verification_summary: The search result provides a list of 10 entries. However, only 5 are specific individuals (Mads Kristensen, Ryan J. Salva, Brian Holt, Matthew J. McCullough, and Charles Black Hammond). The remaining 5 entries are generic job descriptions/open roles rather than specific people. For the individuals listed, they strongly match the criteria for Product Management roles in Seattle focused on developer tools and platforms. Mads Kristensen (Visual Studio) and Ryan Salva (Google Dev Tools) are particularly high-confidence matches.

criteria_results.description: Person is a Full-stack Developer (or equivalent titles like Software Engineer, Full Stack)
criteria_results.evidence: All 10 individuals are explicitly listed with titles such as 'Senior Full Stack Engineer', 'Full Stack Developer', or 'Software Engineer'.

criteria_results.description: Person is located in Austin, Texas
criteria_results.evidence: While most are listed in Austin, Austin Rios is noted as being in Aubrey, TX (which is ~200 miles from Austin), and candidates 9 and 10 are explicitly noted as being in Melbourne and St. Louis respectively.

criteria_results.description: Person has professional experience or proficiency with React
criteria_results.evidence: All 10 profiles explicitly list React or React.js in their titles or bios.

criteria_results.description: Person has professional experience or proficiency with Node.js
criteria_results.evidence: 9 out of 10 profiles (excluding Austin Lancaster) explicitly list Node.js proficiency.

criteria_results.description: Person has professional experience or proficiency with Python
criteria_results.evidence: Only 5 out of 10 candidates (Rios, Light, Destefano, Gooden, Lancaster) are credited with Python experience. Others like Zakvi, Ontiveros, and Burris focus on the MERN stack without Python mentioned.

person_evaluations.verification_summary: The provided data contains a list of 10 individuals. I focused verification on the top candidates who claimed to meet all criteria (React, Node.js, and Python). Austin Light and Joseph Destefano are the strongest matches, explicitly listing all three technologies and the correct location. Austin Rios is a strong match but is located in the Austin 'Metropolitan Area' (specifically Aubrey, TX, which is actually near Dallas, suggesting a potential data hallucination or relocation error in the source). Several other candidates (Zakvi, Ontiveros, Burris) lack Python experience, and some (Lancaster, Long) are no longer in Austin.
